content: Comparative analysis of intrinsic curiosity modules (ICM) and random network distillation (RND) for curiosity-driven reinforcement learning can be reframed as a study of how an agent’s own self-model shapes its exploratory behavior, rather than just a comparison of separate algorithmic designs. This reveals that the performance gap between ICM and RND stems less from their prediction error calculation mechanics and more from how each implicitly structures the agent’s working memory of its own exploratory history.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
